name: Research Data Scotland
description: Research Data Scotland (RDS) is the national coordination and gateway service for accessing linked Scottish public sector data for research. It connects researchers with data from health, social care, education, justice, and other public services across Scotland, facilitating access through the Scottish National Safe Haven.
key_datasets: Scottish Morbidity Records (SMR01 - inpatient, SMR00 - outpatient)
National Records of Scotland (births, deaths, marriages)
Social care (care home census, home care)
linkages: Cross-sector linkage (health, social care, education, justice, housing)

requirements.name: PBPP approval
requirements.description: Application reviewed by the Public Benefit and Privacy Panel. Tier 1 for standard requests, Tier 2 for complex/sensitive data

requirements.name: Safe Haven access
requirements.description: Approved researchers access data only within the Scottish National Safe Haven

requirements.name: Disclosure control
requirements.description: All outputs undergo statistical disclosure control review before release from the Safe Haven

access_steps.name: Initial enquiry with RDS
access_steps.description: Contact Research Data Scotland to discuss research question, data availability, and feasibility

access_steps.name: Prepare and submit PBPP application
access_steps.description: Complete the PBPP application form with study protocol, data requirements, and legal basis

access_steps.name: PBPP review
access_steps.description: Public Benefit and Privacy Panel reviews the application. Tier 1 for standard, Tier 2 for complex requests

access_steps.name: Safe Haven provisioning
access_steps.description: Data prepared and workspace provisioned within the Scottish National Safe Haven

timeline.notes: RDS engagement, feasibility, and application preparation
PBPP review - Tier 1 faster (~6 weeks), Tier 2 longer (~12 weeks)
Data linkage, preparation, and Safe Haven workspace setup

costs.notes: No fees for approved research. RDS is publicly funded to support research using Scottish public sector data.

strengths: Gateway/coordination role simplifying access to multiple Scottish data sources
Rich cross-sector linkage spanning health, social care, education, justice, and housing
Established Scottish National Safe Haven infrastructure
limitations: Scotland only - no data from other UK nations
PBPP review can be lengthy, especially for Tier 2 applications
No data extraction - all analysis within Safe Haven
recent_changes: RDS established as the primary coordination body for Scottish data access
Expanding data partnerships with additional public sector organisations
Improved Safe Haven platform and researcher experience
